diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Polyuria here is largely secondary to fear-driven overdrinking.
Osmotic symptoms can occur in DKA; ketones/symptoms discriminate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Moderate home glucose (~12 mmol/L) with negative blood ketones

investigation_details.investigation_name: Home capillary glucose and blood ketones
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without the home readings, DKA cannot be risk-stratified remotely and the threshold to escalate should be lower.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative ketones with only moderate glucose strongly argue against current DKA.

symptoms.symptom_name: No DKA symptoms (no vomiting/abdominal pain/Kussmaul/drowsiness/fruity breath)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of DKA clinical features argues strongly against ketoacidosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: ~3 L fear-driven water intake explaining the polyuria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large fear-driven water intake explains the secondary polyuria.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home glucose and blood ketone monitoring tonight and repeat if symptomatic
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The key remote risk-stratification tool for DKA in autoimmune diabetes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: GP review of HbA1c and glycaemic management / insulin planning
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: LADA management (timing of insulin) is arranged electively via the GP/endocrinology.
